examples.text: رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ
examples.english: Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear.

etymology.text: From طاق (ṭāq, “arch, doorway, opening in a wall”), from Middle Iranian *tāk (“arch”). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *teg-, and akin to Latin toga (“garment”) and Ancient Greek στέγος (stégos, “roof”).
